tool_args.raw_text: Finance Committee Meeting January 2021. Metropolitan Transportation Authority. 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY 10004. Thursday, 1/21/2021, 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET.

tool_args.label: MTA Finance Committee Members
tool_args.raw_text: Finance Committee Meeting January 2021. Committee Members: L. Schwartz, Chair; A. Albert; J. Barbas; N. Brown; V. Calise; R. Glucksman; R. Herman; D. Jones; L. Lacewell; K. Law; R. Linn; R. Mujica; J. Samuelsen; V. Tessitore; N. Zuckerman.

payload.description: MTA Finance Committee Meeting January 2021. L. Schwartz, Chair. Members: A. Albert, J. Barbas, N. Brown, V. Calise, R. Glucksman, R. Herman, D. Jones, L. Lacewell, K. Law, R. Linn, R. Mujica, J. Samuelsen, V. Tessitore, N. Zuckerman.

tool_args.label: MNR and LIRR Escalator Maintenance and Repair Services
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad ('MNR') requests that the Board adopt a resolution declaring that competitive, sealed bidding is impractical or inappropriate and that it is therefore in the public interest to use the competitive Request for Proposal (RFP) process... for solicitation of maintenance, inspection and repair services for a total of 53 escalators for MNR and Long Island Rail Road ('LIRR') combined.

payload.project_name: Escalator Maintenance, Inspection and Repair Services for MNR and LIRR
payload.description: Request to use the RFP process for maintenance, inspection and repair services for a total of 53 escalators for MNR and LIRR combined. MNR's 15 escalators located in Grand Central Terminal and 2 at White Plains station; LIRR's 20 escalators at various commuter stations and 16 in Penn Station.

tool_args.label: Purchase of 50 Low-Floor 40-foot Diesel Buses for MTABC
tool_args.raw_text: To request that the Board declare competitive bidding impractical or inappropriate for the procurement of 50 low-floor 40-foot diesel buses for MTA Bus Company ('MTABC'), and that it is in the public interest to issue a competitive Request for Proposal ('RFP')...

payload.project_name: Purchase of 50 low-floor 40-foot diesel buses for MTA Bus Company
payload.description: RFP authorization for purchase of 50 low-floor 40-foot diesel buses for MTA Bus Company with an option for 85 additional buses. Replacement of aging buses that have reached end of 12-year useful life. Buses to operate out of MTABC depots in Queens. Features include improved driver visibility, pedestrian turn warning, Wi-Fi, USB charging ports, automatic passenger counters, digital information screens, and new branding.

tool_args.label: Demolition of Former MTA Headquarters Buildings at 347 Madison Avenue
tool_args.raw_text: MTA engaged BP to demolish the former MTA Headquarters Buildings prior to redevelopment due to the deterioration of the vacated Buildings, life safety considerations... The initial budget estimate at that time was $25 million with demolition anticipated to be completed in 2020... After a thorough demolition design and bidding process... the final budget is now set at $34 million. The revised schedule projects that demolition should be completed in the 2nd quarter of 2022.

payload.project_name: Demolition of former MTA Headquarters Buildings at 341, 345, 347 Madison Avenue
payload.description: Demolition of former MTA Headquarters Buildings at 341, 345, and 347 Madison Avenue, New York, NY. Initially budgeted at $25 million, final budget revised to $34 million. Demolition anticipated to be completed in Q2 2022. BP 347 Madison Associates (Boston Properties) selected as designated developer through 2013 RFP.

tool_args.label: MTA Automated Vehicle Location Monitoring System
tool_args.raw_text: Board approval is sought to award a competitively negotiated miscellaneous services contract to LB Technology Inc. ('LB') for implementation of Automated Vehicle Location Monitoring System (AVLM) for all MTA Agencies for up to seven (7) years. This includes delivery and installation of all hardware, cloud-based software, wireless data service, maintenance and support services in the not-to-exceed amount of $4,026,393.

payload.description: Implementation of MTA Automated Vehicle Location Monitoring System for all MTA Agencies. Includes delivery and installation of all hardware, cloud-based software, wireless data service, maintenance and support services in the not-to-exceed amount of $4,026,393. Replaces current AVLM system for non-passenger fleet of approximately 2,500 vehicles.

tool_args.label: MTA Payroll Mobility Tax BANs Series 2020A Issuance
tool_args.raw_text: On December 17, 2020, MTA issued $2,907.280 million of MTA Payroll Mobility Tax Bond Anticipation Notes, Series 2020A... The Series 2020A notes were issued as fixed rate tax-exempt notes with a final maturity of December 15, 2023 and an all-in True Interest Cost of 1.356%.

payload.description: MTA issued $2,907.280 million of MTA Payroll Mobility Tax Bond Anticipation Notes, Series 2020A. Fixed rate tax-exempt notes, final maturity December 15, 2023, all-in True Interest Cost of 1.356%. Proceeds used to finance COVID-19 Lost Revenues and Increased Costs.

tool_args.label: Diesel Fuel Hedge Executed December 2020
tool_args.raw_text: On December 31, 2020, MTA executed a 2,826,765 gallon ultra-low sulfur diesel fuel hedge with J.P. Morgan Ventures Energy Corporation at an all-in price of $1.5355/gallon.

payload.description: MTA executed a 2,826,765 gallon ultra-low sulfur diesel fuel hedge with J.P. Morgan Ventures Energy Corporation at an all-in price of $1.5355/gallon. Covers period December 2021 through November 2022.

tool_args.label: Lease Agreement with Swarovski at Grand Central Terminal
tool_args.raw_text: Authorization to enter into a lease agreement with Swarovski Retail Ventures Ltd. for retail space MC-32 on the Main Concourse in Grand Central Terminal ('GCT'). TERM: 5 years. SPACE: 590 sq. ft.

payload.description: Authorization to enter into a 5-year lease agreement with Swarovski Retail Ventures Ltd. for retail space MC-32 on the Main Concourse in Grand Central Terminal. 590 sq. ft. Year 1 guaranteed annual minimum rent of $100,000 with percentage rent.

tool_args.label: Lease Agreement with Hudson Group Retail at Grand Central Terminal
tool_args.raw_text: Authorization to enter into a lease agreement with Hudson Group (HG) Retail LLC for retail space MC-21 on the Main Concourse in Grand Central Terminal ('GCT'). TERM: 5 years. SPACE: 2,327 sq. ft. Year 1 Guaranteed Annual Minimum Rent $535,000.

payload.description: Authorization to enter into a 5-year lease agreement with Hudson Group (HG) Retail LLC for retail space MC-21 on the Main Concourse in Grand Central Terminal. 2,327 sq. ft. Year 1 guaranteed annual minimum rent of $535,000 with percentage rent.

tool_args.raw_text: Sammarco has occupied the Location since 1966 and operates a stone, masonry and building supply business and their main facility abuts the Location.

tool_args.raw_text: Permittee: SHR Group, Inc. Activity: Entry Permit for the closure of the easternmost lane of traffic, and the installation, maintenance, and subsequent removal of a scaffold and associated Maintenance and Protection of Traffic equipment for Local Law 11 façade repair work.

payload.description: Designated developer (selected through 2013 RFP) for redevelopment of former MTA Headquarters Buildings at 341, 345, 347 Madison Avenue. Also d/b/a Boston Properties. Engaged by MTA via single source personal services contract to manage demolition of the Buildings.

tool_args.label: AVLM Contract Savings Compared to Existing
tool_args.raw_text: Under the current contracts for various agencies, MTA is paying $6.1 million for a 7-year term. Therefore, LB's price of $4M is 34% ($2M) below the existing contracts. LB's price is also 84% less than that of the highest finalist.
